source_document:
REMOTE WORK AND EXPENSE POLICY (Effective January 1, 2025)

1. Eligible employees: Full-time staff only; contractors are not eligible for the remote stipend or annual equipment reimbursement.
2. Remote work: Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week without extra approval. A 4th remote day in the same week requires written manager approval received before that week begins.
3. Annual home-office stipend: Up to $500 per calendar year for approved expenses; amounts above $500 are not reimbursed.
4. Submission deadline: Expense reports must be submitted by the 15th day of the month following the purchase. Reports received after the 15th are denied for that purchase period.
5. Support response time: Non-urgent tickets receive a first response within 2 business days; urgent (Severity 1) tickets require a first response within 4 business hours.
6. Equipment: Company laptops remain company property and must be returned within 10 business days after employment ends; failure to return may result in payroll deduction up to the replacement cost.

examples.question: How many remote days are allowed each week without approval?
examples.answer: You can work remotely up to 3 days per week without extra approval.

examples.question: What is the annual cap on home-office reimbursement?
examples.answer: The policy reimburses up to $500 per calendar year for approved home-office expenses.

examples.question: Who is eligible for the remote stipend?
examples.answer: Only full-time staff are eligible; contractors are not eligible for the remote stipend.

examples.question: When must expense reports be submitted?
examples.answer: Expense reports must be submitted by the 15th day of the month following the purchase.

examples.question: What if I want a fourth remote day in one week?
examples.answer: A fourth remote day requires written manager approval before that week begins.

examples.question: How fast should urgent support tickets get a first response?
examples.answer: Urgent Severity 1 tickets require a first response within 4 business hours.

examples.question: Are amounts above the annual stipend limit reimbursed?
examples.answer: No; amounts above $500 are not reimbursed under this policy.

examples.question: What happens to company laptops after employment ends?
examples.answer: Company laptops must be returned within 10 business days after employment ends.

examples.question: What is the first-response SLA for non-urgent tickets?
examples.answer: Non-urgent tickets receive a first response within 2 business days.

examples.question: What happens if I submit my expense report late?
examples.answer: Reports received after the 15th are denied for that purchase period.

examples.question: How many remote days are allowed each week without approval?
examples.answer: You can work remotely up to 5 days per week without any approval.

examples.question: What is the annual cap on home-office reimbursement?
examples.answer: The annual cap is $1,200 per employee.

examples.question: Are contractors eligible for the annual equipment reimbursement?
examples.answer: Yes, contractors are eligible for the full $500 annual reimbursement.

examples.question: When must expense reports be submitted?
examples.answer: You must submit by the last day of the month; there is no grace period.

examples.question: What if I want a fourth remote day in one week?
examples.answer: A fourth day is always allowed without manager approval.

examples.question: How fast should urgent support tickets get a first response?
examples.answer: Urgent tickets can wait up to 2 business days for a first response.

examples.question: Is there reimbursement above the $500 cap if I have receipts?
examples.answer: Yes, with manager approval you can be reimbursed above $500.

examples.question: When do I return my laptop after leaving the company?
examples.answer: Laptops may be kept personally; return is optional within 30 days.

examples.question: What is the first-response time for non-urgent tickets?
examples.answer: Non-urgent tickets are answered within 4 business hours.

examples.question: Does the policy allow remote work at all?
examples.answer: No, remote work is not permitted under any circumstances.

examples.question: How many remote days and what is the stipend cap?
examples.answer: You get 3 remote days per week and up to $500 per year, and contractors get the same stipend as full-time staff.

examples.question: What are the expense rules?
examples.answer: Submit by the 15th of the following month; the annual cap is $500.

examples.question: What happens with a fourth remote day?
examples.answer: You need manager approval, but it can be verbal anytime during the week.

examples.question: Urgent vs non-urgent support SLAs?
examples.answer: Urgent tickets get a response within 4 business hours; non-urgent details are not specified in the policy.

examples.question: Who gets the $500 reimbursement?
examples.answer: Full-time staff can receive up to $500; contractor eligibility is not mentioned.

examples.question: Laptop return after termination?
examples.answer: You must return the laptop within 10 business days; keeping it has no financial consequences.

examples.question: Is $600 reimbursable if I split it across two years?
examples.answer: The policy sets a $500 per calendar year cap; splitting purchases does not change the annual limit.

examples.question: Deadline for January purchases?
examples.answer: Submit by the 15th of the following month, which for January would be February 15.

examples.question: Can I work remotely 3 days without approval?
examples.answer: Yes, up to 3 days; for more days you always need approval, with no timing rules.

examples.question: Are expenses over $500 ever covered?
examples.answer: The document states amounts above $500 are not reimbursed; there is no exception listed.
